transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thanks for calling Riverbend Health Navigation. What's going on today?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Hi, I've had this sharp pain in my lower right belly since last night. It's getting worse.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'm sorry you're dealing with that. On a scale of one to ten, how severe is the pain right now?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Maybe an eight. It hurts more when I press on it or move around.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Have you had any fever, nausea, or vomiting along with the pain?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Yeah, I threw up once this morning and I feel a bit feverish.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That combination, especially pain that's worsening in the lower right abdomen, could suggest something like appendicitis. This needs evaluation right away.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Oh no. Should I just go to urgent care?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: No, I'd recommend going straight to the emergency room, not urgent care, since this could require immediate imaging or surgery. Is someone able to drive you?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Yes, my husband's home. He can take me now.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good. Please go now, and mention the fever, vomiting, and pain location to the ER team right away. Do you want me to note this in your file for your primary doctor?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Yes, please do that. Thank you for the help.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're welcome, I've added the note. Take care, and I hope you feel better soon.
